summary: The team is in full sprint mode to deliver the joint CKGSB-IE report 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' by early September, with the Asia Start campaign and Intelligent China program also launching imminently.
summaryDetail: The single most important initiative is the joint report with IE on Chinese companies going global, which must be finalized by early September for launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore. Felix is preparing a one-pager proposal, Julie is sourcing alumni articles, and the team is translating the Chinese report. This week, confirm launch events with Miguel from Tech In Asia and finalize the report structure. Simultaneously, the Asia Start campaign is launching with two routes (AI and traditional) — Daisy and Joseph are leading execution, with the first month's content going live now. The Intelligent China program, a new in-depth English program for foreigners, needs its website ready and SCM campaign started; module one is Dec 1-4 in Beijing. Additionally, several editorial items are due: revise the 'Disruptive Timing' article title, confirm the Dean's article title, review the joint report plan, and check overlap between Professor Li Wei's FT piece and the report. Urgent this week: schedule the Tech In Asia meeting, finalize the report proposal, and get the Intelligent China website live.

meetings.title: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global
meetings.summary: Meeting to plan a joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with op-eds from both schools' leadership and industry case studies. Report to be launched at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore in mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with module one in Beijing in December.

meetings.title: Europe-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on geopolitical tensions affecting European companies in China, with emphasis on de-risking and the role of companies versus governments in EU-China relations.

decisions.text: Asia Start campaign will run two routes for first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program targets foreigners, is in English, and module one is Dec 1-4 in Beijing.
Proceed with the joint report on Chinese companies going global with IE, with Professor Li Wei's collaboration.
Use the title with 'China's gateway' for the Dean's article.
